gaps.why_it_matters: prune deletes from self._data while iterating it — the first matching entry raises RuntimeError: dictionary changed size during iteration; zero tests call prune
gaps.suggested_test: populate 3 entries, prune(lambda v: True): assert behavior (currently raises RuntimeError) and pin the intended contract

gaps.why_it_matters: The oldest-INSERTED contract is untested around re-puts: updating an existing key keeps its original position, so the next overflow evicts an entry callers just refreshed
gaps.suggested_test: capacity=2: put a, put b, put a again, put c — assert which key was evicted matches the documented contract
